persona.name: Keisha Simmons

occupation.title: Marketing Director
occupation.organization: Covenant Health Plans (regional health insurance company), Charlotte, NC
occupation.description: Leads a 9-person marketing team responsible for member acquisition campaigns, employer group partnerships, and brand identity for a mid-sized regional insurer. High-stakes presentations to C-suite and external agencies are a regular part of her week. Sharp eye for brand inconsistency — applies the same scrutiny to running shoe advertising she gives to her own team's work.

persona.education: B.S. Marketing, Howard University; M.B.A., Wake Forest University
long_term_goals: Maintain a consistent running habit as a cornerstone of her health through menopause
Travel to all 7 continents — at 4 so far
Teach her two kids that healthy habits are built slowly, not hacked
persona.style: Polished and deliberate in professional settings; warm, candid, and slightly irreverent with people she trusts. Thinks out loud in pros/cons before committing to an opinion. Communicates in full, well-structured thoughts. Not a pushover — will push back on a bad product diplomatically but persistently. Her brand instincts show when she evaluates shoes: she notices packaging, in-store presentation, and color system before she notices price.

traits: Brand-aware in everyday life — evaluates consumer experiences instinctively
Practical and results-oriented; wants things to work without requiring effort
Community-minded and generous with professional mentorship
Low tolerance for overpromise/underdeliver, in business or product purchases

interests: Running 3x per week for stress management and cardiovascular health
Hiking with her family on Uwharrie National Forest trails
Cooking — Southern classics and West African-inspired dishes
National park travel as a family priority
Brand strategy and consumer behavior podcasts
Home renovation (currently converting a basement into a home gym)
likes: Wide toe-box shoes that don't blister her on long runs
Brands with strong loyalty programs she actually uses
Consistent neighborhood running routes she can do on autopilot after hard workdays
Group fitness classes (Orangetheory) on rest days
Brooks and New Balance for reliable, unfussy daily training
dislikes: Shoes that cause blisters within the first mile — she returns them immediately
Overcomplicated buying processes (confusing sizing, poor return policies)
Brands that over-promise performance in their marketing
Shoes with ugly colorways even if the fit is good — she will not wear them
Being upsold at running stores by people who assume she's a beginner

beliefs: Health is wealth — neglect your body and it will demand attention at the worst time
Representation in brand imagery matters and she will spend accordingly
You don't have to run fast or far to be a runner
A good buying experience earns more loyalty than a good product alone
skills: Brand strategy and consumer marketing campaign management
Running for consistency (has maintained a 3x/week habit for 6 years through job changes and two pregnancies)
Shoe fit evaluation from a comfort-and-wear standpoint
Mentoring Black women in mid-career marketing transitions

general: Jogs 3 mornings per week (typically Tue/Thu/Sat) at 5:45am before the household wakes
Buys shoes based on peer recommendations first, brand reputation second, and online reviews as tiebreaker
Owns 2–3 pairs simultaneously — one fresh, one worn, one retired to the treadmill
Evaluates running shoes the same way she evaluates brand campaigns: what does this promise, does the experience match the promise?

morning: 3–4 mile neighborhood jog — same routes, same pace
Coffee and email review before kids wake at 7am
workday: In the office or on calls by 8:30am
Lunch usually eaten at desk; takes a 20-minute walk when possible
Decompresses after 6pm by listening to a podcast on the drive home
evening: Family dinner together — fairly strict rule about phones
Helps with homework and bedtime routines
Watches one episode of something she chose, not the kids' shows
weekend: Long run or brisk walk Saturday morning with a neighbor friend
Family outing Saturday afternoon (trails, a museum, or farmers' market)

health.physical: Generally healthy; jogs 9–12 miles per week. Has mild pronation and wears stability shoes on podiatrist's advice. Manages minor knee stiffness with post-run stretching. Energy and fitness are priorities, not speed or distance goals.
health.mental: Running is her primary decompression valve after high-cognitive-load workdays. Good support network through close friends and her church community. Occasional stress about career trajectory and work-life balance management.

relationships.name: Derek Simmons
relationships.description: High school football coach and PE teacher. Understands athletic training rhythms; picks up morning kid duties on her running days without complaint.

relationships.name: Jasmine
relationships.description: Lives in Atlanta. They do virtual 5Ks together through a running app and text each other splits with competitive trash talk.

relationships.name: Linda Simmons
relationships.description: Lives in Richmond, VA. Calls weekly; was the one who told Keisha in her 30s that running would save her sanity — she was right.
